Assemble a Model 2a covariance matrix

Source: R/build_sigma_2a.R
build_sigma_2a.Rd

Builds the 2P x 2P Model 2a (Chapter 1 + alpha) covariance matrix from two within-biomarker blocks and a diagonal cross-biomarker block: $$\Sigma = \begin{pmatrix} \Sigma_G & C \\ C^\top & \Sigma_A \end{pmatrix}, \quad C = \mathrm{diag}(c_1, \ldots, c_P).$$ Setting c_vec = 0 recovers the Chapter 1 block-diagonal covariance, so Model 2a strictly nests Chapter 1.

This is a small pure helper used by sim_params_2a() and the tests; it does no model fitting.

Usage

build_sigma_2a(sigma_g, sigma_a, c_vec)

Arguments

sigma_g

A P x P within-biomarker covariance for biomarker 1 (e.g. IgG). Must be symmetric positive-definite.

sigma_a

A P x P within-biomarker covariance for biomarker 2 (e.g. IgA). Must be symmetric positive-definite.

c_vec

A length-P numeric vector of same-parameter cross-biomarker covariances (the diagonal of C).

Value

A 2P x 2P symmetric covariance matrix. Errors if the result is not positive-definite (i.e. the requested cross-covariances are too large).

On this page

Developed by Peter Teunis, Samuel Schildhauer, Kwan Ho Lee, Kristen Aiemjoy, Douglas Ezra Morrison.
